---
title: "Sorghum V5: Negative ZSS Distribution Across Genomic Features"
author: "Subhash"
date: "2026-03-23"
output: html_document
---

## Goal

Map **negative ZSS SNPs** to Sorghum V5 genomic features and build a clean table for plotting ZSS distribution by feature.

We keep ZSS as negative values (no absolute transform in this workflow).

## Input Files (HCC)

- `zeroscore.bed`  
  Path: `/mnt/nrdstor/jyanglab/subhash/datasets/3.results/negative/zeroscore.bed`  
  Expected columns: `chr start end pos zss`

- `sorghum_v5_gene_features_2kb_labeled.chr.bed`  
  Path: `/mnt/nrdstor/jyanglab/subhash/datasets/3.results/negative/genomic_fetures/sorghum_v5_gene_features_2kb_labeled.chr.bed`  
  Expected columns: `chr start end feature gene_id strand`

## Why Duplicate Handling Is Needed

A SNP can overlap more than one feature (for example exon and gene_body).  
If we keep all overlaps, one SNP can be counted multiple times, which biases feature-wise counts and distributions.

So we assign **one SNP -> one feature** with priority:

`exon > intron > upstream_2kb > downstream_2kb > gene_body`

## Step-by-Step Commands (HCC)

```bash
cd /mnt/nrdstor/jyanglab/subhash/datasets/3.results/negative
```

### 1) Keep only negative ZSS SNPs

```{bash}
awk 'BEGIN{FS="[[:space:]]+"; OFS="\t"}
     NF>=5 && $2~/^[0-9]+$/ && $3~/^[0-9]+$/ && $5<0 {
       print $1, int($2), int($3), int($4), $5, $1 ":" int($4)
     }' zeroscore.bed \
| sort -k1,1 -k2,2n > zss_neg.sorted.bed
```

Output columns:

`chr start end pos zss snpid`

### 2) Clean/sort feature BED

```bash
awk 'BEGIN{FS="[[:space:]]+"; OFS="\t"}
     NF>=6 && $2~/^[0-9]+$/ && $3~/^[0-9]+$/ {
       print $1, int($2), int($3), $4, $5, $6
     }' genomic_fetures/sorghum_v5_gene_features_2kb_labeled.chr.bed \
| sort -k1,1 -k2,2n > genomic_fetures/features.sorted.bed
```

### 3) Intersect negative SNPs with features

```bash
bedtools intersect \
  -a zss_neg.sorted.bed \
  -b genomic_fetures/features.sorted.bed \
  -wa -wb > zss_neg_feature_overlap.tsv
```

### 4) Keep one feature per SNP using priority

```bash
awk 'BEGIN{FS=OFS="\t";
  p["exon"]=1; p["intron"]=2; p["upstream_2kb"]=3; p["downstream_2kb"]=4; p["gene_body"]=5}
{
  id=$6; f=$10; pr=p[f];
  if(!(id in best) || pr<bp[id]){best[id]=$0; bp[id]=pr}
}
END{for(i in best) print best[i]}' zss_neg_feature_overlap.tsv > zss_neg_feature_unique.tsv
```

### 5) Build compact plotting table

```bash
awk 'BEGIN{FS=OFS="\t"} {print $1,$4,$5,$10,$11}' zss_neg_feature_unique.tsv \
> zss_neg_by_feature.tsv
```

Output columns:

`chr pos zss feature gene_id`

### 6) QC checks

```bash
wc -l zss_neg.sorted.bed zss_neg_feature_overlap.tsv zss_neg_feature_unique.tsv
cut -f4 zss_neg_by_feature.tsv | sort | uniq -c
```

## Interpretation of Your Current Counts

From your output:

- `downstream_2kb`: 583,740
- `exon`: 1,100,930
- `intron`: 972,549
- `upstream_2kb`: 845,601

Total assigned negative SNPs in these features: **3,502,820**.

Total negative SNPs in `zss_neg.sorted.bed`: **13,472,877**.

So SNPs outside these annotated feature classes are:

`13,472,877 - 3,502,820 = 9,970,057`.

## Files to Keep for Plotting

Minimum needed to plot ZSS distributions by feature:

- `zss_neg_by_feature.tsv`  (required)

Recommended to keep for reproducibility:

- `zss_neg_feature_unique.tsv`
- `zss_neg_feature_overlap.tsv`
- `genomic_fetures/features.sorted.bed`

## Minimal R Plot Example

```{r}
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)

d <- fread("zss_neg_by_feature.tsv", col.names = c("chr","pos","zss","feature","gene_id"))
d[, feature := factor(feature, levels = c("exon","intron","upstream_2kb","downstream_2kb","gene_body"))]
d <- d[!is.na(feature)]

ggplot(d, aes(x = feature, y = zss, fill = feature)) +
  geom_violin(trim = TRUE, scale = "width", color = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, outlier.size = 0.2, fill = "white") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Negative zero-shot score (ZSS)")
```
